concept: Consensus / The Presence of a Shared Mind
description: A general agreement arrived at by a group. It is not a simple majority, but a collective decision that all members consent to and support, reached through inclusive and participatory dialogue.
rationale: A composition of the roots 'we' (the collective), 'no' (mind), and 'a' (presence). The word's structure suggests 'the presence of a shared mind,' defining consensus as a state where the group thinks and feels as one. The vowel pair 'o.a' gives it a harmonious, unified sound.
